audit_items.proof_trace: All four RpcChannel constructors (lines 19, 31, 38, 46) call rpc_client->impl() without any prior null check on the raw rpc_client pointer, directly violating the invariant that every dereferenced pointer p satisfies p != NULL. No DCHECK, ASSERT, or conditional guard exists anywhere in rpc_channel.cc to enforce non-null rpc_client. The _impl shared_ptr is always initialized via standard new (which throws rather than returns null), so _impl->Stop()/_impl->CallMethod() are safe post-construction, but the rpc_client dereferences are not.
audit_items.attack_scenario: If application code—driven by network-influenced service-discovery or factory logic—constructs an RpcChannel with a null rpc_client, the process crashes at constructor time via null pointer dereference, enabling a remote denial-of-service against any server using this pattern.
